markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Denis Shapovalov win the 2026 Men's French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's implied 67.5% probability as the clear French Open favorite stems from his dominant clay-court surge, including a Monte Carlo Masters 1000 title over Carlos Alcaraz on April 13 that reclaimed the World No. 1 ranking amid a 17-match winning streak with just one set dropped. Alcaraz's recent right wrist injury withdrawal from Roland Garros—announced last week—plummets his chances to 0.4%, reshaping the draw and elevating Sinner as top seed. Alexander Zverev (6.3%) and Novak Djokovic (6.2%) trail as proven clay contenders with strong rankings and historical deep runs, though Djokovic's age and Zverev's head-to-head deficits temper expectations in this wide-open field beyond the leader.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors retired Army Maj. Gen. Bert Mizusawa as the frontrunner in Virginia's Republican U.S. Senate primary at 52% implied probability, driven by his leading fundraising of approximately $192,000 through Q1 2026 and decades of military command, legislative oversight, and executive experience that appeal to GOP primary voters eyeing a formidable challenger to incumbent Democrat Mark Warner's reelection bid. David Williams holds second at 21.5%, leveraging over 30 years of Navy, Marine Corps, and State Department service, while Kim Farington sits at 15.8% following Republican Party of Virginia certification of the top three for the August 4 ballot. Alex De Paula trails at 14.1% amid scant fundraising below $6,000; no public polls exist, leaving odds sensitive to forthcoming endorsements, debates, or campaign events before the primary.

markets.question: Will Khvicha Kvaratskhelia be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé leads the UEFA Champions League 2025/26 top scorer race with 15 goals in 11 matches for Real Madrid, fueling trader consensus at 59.5% implied probability as the frontrunner, bolstered by his clinical 1.25 goals-per-game rate and Real Madrid's strong position in the semi-finals. Harry Kane trails closely with 13 goals across 12 appearances for Bayern Munich, commanding 39.5% odds after narrowing the gap with a key strike in the first leg of Bayern's semi-final against PSG three days ago, highlighting his consistent finishing amid a grueling knockout schedule. Khvicha Kvaratskhelia sits distant third at around 10 goals for PSG, buoyed by a recent leveler versus Bayern but hindered by the two-goal deficit to the leaders, while others like Julian Alvarez linger far behind following eliminations or stalled form in the latter stages.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30, reflecting the absence of any announced summits or bilateral visits within the resolution window, despite ongoing phone diplomacy. A Kremlin-initiated call on April 29 discussed Ukraine ceasefire prospects and potential sanctions relief, building on their August 2025 Alaska summit, but produced no face-to-face commitment. Reports of a U.S. invitation for Putin to the December 2026 G20 in Miami emerged April 23, yet fall outside the deadline. Ukraine's eroding trust in U.S. mediation, stalled peace talks, and logistical hurdles for neutral-site diplomacy keep alternatives like China or Russia at low probabilities, with late-breaking escalations or breakthroughs needed to shift odds.

markets.question: Spread: SC Freiburg (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Freiburg" if SC Freiburg win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Freiburg vs. VfL Wolfsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Freiburg holds a strong position in eighth place, riding a solid run of three recent wins and aiming to solidify mid-table security with home advantage at Europa-Park Stadion against relegation-threatened VfL Wolfsburg, who sit 17th amid a winless streak and just one victory in their last five matches. Wolfsburg's woes deepened yesterday with captain Maximilian Arnold ruled out due to a lingering groin injury, joining key absences like Jonas Wind (hamstring), Jenson Seelt (knee), and Kevin Paredes (muscle), thinning their squad for this survival scrap. Freiburg boasts balanced head-to-head history (14 wins to Wolfsburg's 13) and robust home form, while Wolfsburg's away struggles and injury crisis tilt trader consensus toward the hosts in this penultimate matchday clash.

markets.question: Will SpaceX IPO by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If SpaceX is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 33°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors 32°C (99.7%) as Taipei's highest temperature on May 2, backed by Central Weather Administration (CWA) observational data from Taipei Songshan Airport showing an early peak of 32°C under mostly clear skies and a stable high-pressure system. This aligns with recent forecast model runs projecting daytime highs near 30–33°C apparent temperatures amid low precipitation odds (around 20–30%), enhanced by strong solar insolation and minimal cloud interference typical for early May's transitioning climatology. Historical May averages hover at 28–29°C, but current southerly flows have boosted warmth. Scenarios challenging this include an improbable late-afternoon heat burst from sudden clearing or measurement revision, though sunset nears and diurnal cooling dominates; watch CWA's end-of-day update for confirmation.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

events.title: Which artists will have a Billboard #1 song this year?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo secured her 2026 Billboard Hot 100 #1 with "Drop Dead" debuting atop the chart dated May 2, cementing trader consensus on her outcome amid massive streaming debut and promotional rollout from her latest album. Earlier frontrunners like Ella Langley ("Choosin' Texas," seven-week reign ending recently), Bruno Mars ("I Just Might"), Taylor Swift ("Opalite"), and BTS ("Swim") have already locked in yes resolutions, reflecting country-pop crossovers and K-pop resurgence driving early-year momentum. With summer releases looming from artists like Post Malone and Sabrina Carpenter, plus weekly chart updates tracking sales, airplay, and streams, markets remain fluid—watch for debut-heavy strategies and viral TikTok trajectories to propel challengers.

markets.question: Will Do Jong-hwan win the 2026 Chungcheongbuk Province gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Chungcheongbuk Province gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in South Korea on June 3, 2026 to elect the next governor of Chungcheongbuk Province. 

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

With campaigns intensifying ahead of early voting, potential shifts could arise from scandals, strong debate showings by Kim, or abrupt national political reversals altering voter turnout in this closely watched provincial race.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Will Israel win the televote for Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the televote in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

markets.question: Ethereum Up or Down - May 2, 2:20AM-2:25AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's early April 2026 threats to withdraw the US from NATO, citing allies' refusal to join the US-Israel conflict against Iran, drove a brief surge in speculation, with White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt confirming internal discussions ahead of a meeting with NATO Secretary General Jens Stoltenberg. However, Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act bars unilateral presidential withdrawal, mandating congressional approval or a two-thirds Senate vote amid bipartisan opposition from figures like Sen. Tim Kaine. No legislative action has advanced since, and Stoltenberg recently deemed it unlikely. Trader consensus reflects entrenched legal and institutional barriers, ongoing Ukraine aid debates, and European pledges to boost defense spending as factors maintaining alliance stability through 2026.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C at the Observatory headquarters, placing it firmly in the 25°C or higher range and driving trader consensus to 99.9% implied probability. This outcome aligns with an exceptionally warm April overall, featuring a monthly peak of 30.2°C on April 16 amid southerly airstreams and abundant sunshine earlier in the month, though a late northeast monsoon introduced cloudier conditions and cooler mornings toward month's end. Climatologically, late April maxima average 27–28°C, but 2026's elevated temperatures reflect above-normal regional heat. Scenarios challenging this near-certainty—such as a rare data revision from equipment calibration issues—are highly improbable given HKO's rigorous measurement standards, with final resolution pending any confirmatory updates.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon's share price closed at $268.26 on May 1, 2026, up 27% over the past month as the top-performing Magnificent Seven stock, propelled by Q1 earnings released April 29 that beat expectations with $181.5 billion in net sales (up 17% year-over-year) and EPS of $2.78 versus $1.63 consensus. AWS revenue surged 28% on AI-driven demand, offsetting e-commerce margin pressures amid $200 billion capex plans. Consensus analyst price targets average $310, signaling trader optimism for revenue growth and cloud dominance. Watch June's earlier-than-usual Prime Day sales event and macroeconomic factors like Treasury yields influencing tech valuations, with resolution hinging on May-end closing price.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

markets.question: Will Russia enter Shevchenko by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Shevchenko, Donetsk Oblast, (48.384157880601364° N, 37.10809761679785° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Institute for the Study of War reports highlight ongoing clashes near Hryshyne and Shevchenko through mid-April, with recent geolocated footage showing heavy Russian infiltration losses and flanking attempts around Rodynske. Trader consensus implies low probability of near-term capture due to Ukrainian drone strikes disrupting reinforcements and Pokrovsk axis stalemate, despite Moscow's manpower commitments. Upcoming frontline escalations or aid deliveries could shift dynamics, with resolution tied to ISW maps confirming territorial control.

A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 58-59°F on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Los Angeles International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 50.5% implied probability for a highest temperature of 68-69°F at Los Angeles International Airport (KLAX) on May 2, driven by the National Weather Service's latest guidance showing persistent marine layer stratus clouds under weak onshore flow, capping coastal highs in the upper 60s—2-4°F below the 69°F climatological normal. Yesterday's observed high near 67°F amid similar low-cloud cover reinforces this pattern, with forecast models like GFS and ECMWF indicating limited afternoon burn-off due to a shallow marine inversion. Key uncertainty lies in the timing and extent of any clearing; sustained cloudiness could favor 66-67°F (32%), while earlier sunshine might push toward 70°F. Monitor hourly METAR observations at KLAX for real-time shifts as the market nears resolution this evening.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Realistic challenges include prolonged procedural delays, election disputes, or unexpected health events for other leaders accelerating their exits.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 closed at 7,230 on May 1, up roughly 5.6% year-to-date, propelled by positive Q1 earnings revisions—now projecting 12-17% growth for 2026—fueled by AI-driven sectors and resilient consumer spending. March unemployment held at 4.3%, Q1 GDP expanded 2.0%, and inflation showed signs of moderation per the latest CPI data, supporting the Federal Reserve's steady stance at its April 28-29 FOMC meeting amid market-implied odds for potential mid-year rate cuts. Analyst consensus targets year-end levels around 7,600-7,750, implying modest upside, though traders eye volatility risks from upcoming April CPI on May 12, June FOMC deliberations, and Q2 earnings season.

markets.question: Spread: FC Lorient (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Lorient" if FC Lorient win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Paris Saint-Germain FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. FC Lorient - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain top the Ligue 1 table with 63 points from 27 matches, holding a game in hand over challengers Lens ahead of hosting ninth-placed FC Lorient (43 points from 29 games) on May 2 at Parc des Princes. Trader sentiment favors PSG due to their strong home form, dominant head-to-head record (21 wins in 35 meetings), and recent Ligue 1 victory over Toulouse (3-1), despite UCL demands and injuries sidelining Nuno Mendes (muscle) and Timothée Pembele (knee). Lorient's momentum from a shock 2-0 upset over fourth-placed Marseille on April 18 is tempered by key absences like Théo Le Bris (knee, early May return) and Mohamed Talbi (calf doubt), plus coach Olivier Pantaloni's announced season-end departure introducing uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Iván Cepeda Castro place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent late-April polls, including AtlasIntel (April 25-29) showing Abelardo de la Espriella at 29.4% and Paloma Valencia at 20.9%, alongside close Guarumo results (23.9% vs. 22.8%), position de la Espriella as the trader-favored contender for second place behind frontrunner Iván Cepeda's 37-44% across Invamer, GAD3, and others. Valencia's momentum from her strong March 8 primaries performance has plateaued, while de la Espriella consolidates far-right support amid right-wing vote fragmentation. Polymarket consensus prices this polling edge at 51% for de la Espriella versus 38.5% for Valencia, with the May 31 first-round outcome hinging on undecideds, turnout in battleground regions, and potential consolidations before the runoff.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 24°C on May 2?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects EUR/USD's recent slide below 1.1730 to around 1.1720, driven by Federal Reserve's decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29 meeting amid persistent 3.3% March CPI inflation, widening the policy gap with the ECB's unchanged 2.15% main refinancing rate and 2.00% deposit facility. Eurozone April CPI flashed at 3.0%, up from 2.6% in March, hinting at potential ECB hawkishness, yet dollar strength persists on Middle East tensions and robust U.S. data. Key catalysts include May 12 U.S. April CPI release, June 10 ECB meeting, and June 16-17 FOMC, with forecasts eyeing 1.20-1.28 by year-end amid narrowing rate differentials.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump visit China on May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the calendar date (ET) on which U.S. President Donald Trump next visits China.

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

No visit by May 31 trades low at 11.5%, reflecting low cancellation risk despite geopolitical uncertainties around trade, Taiwan, and regional stability.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds target range steady at 3.5%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid hotter-than-expected March PCE inflation rising to 3.5% year-over-year (headline) and 3.2% core, up from prior months. This reflects trader consensus pricing in persistent price pressures despite solid GDP growth projections of 2.4% for 2026 and unemployment edging to 4.4%, per the March dot plot forecasting median rates at 3.4% year-end 2026 and 3.1% in 2027. With 10-year Treasury yields near 4.4%, upcoming catalysts include April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, where labor data like March's 178,000 nonfarm payrolls could signal easing or renewed hawkishness shaping the rate path before 2027.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93% implied probability against direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, driven by Pyongyang's repeated rejections of Seoul's overtures and prioritization of military escalation over diplomacy. North Korea's April 19 ballistic missile tests and deepening defense ties with Russia—highlighted by Kim Jong Un's late-April meeting with Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov—underscore its focus on nuclear expansion and alliances outside the peninsula. Earlier gestures, like South Korean President Lee Jae-myung's April apology for a drone incursion, elicited a positive response from Kim Yo Jong but led nowhere, with North Korea dismissing inter-Korean dialogue as infeasible on April 8. Seoul's unification minister sought EU mediation for indirect "two-plus-one" talks on April 1, signaling no bilateral progress amid no scheduled high-level meetings. A sudden breakthrough would require major concessions, but current hostilities make this improbable.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation remains tightly contested, with implied probabilities clustered around 1.8–2.0% (41.9%), 2.1–2.3% (37.5%), and 2.7–2.9% (37.4%), reflecting uncertainty over the trajectory amid recent upticks. March 2026 CPI rose to 2.2% year-over-year—up from 2.0% in prior months but below the 2.4% forecast—driven by energy costs from Middle East tensions and 16% import price surges, while core CPI eased to 2.3%. Key differentiators include April CPI data (due imminently) and the May 28 BoK meeting, where won weakness or oil volatility could tip odds higher.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" US-Cuba military clash in 2026 at 56%, reflecting heightened but contained tensions amid ongoing diplomacy and congressional checks. Pentagon contingency planning accelerated in mid-April following President Trump's hints at post-Iran intervention and new oil sanctions, prompting Cuban President Díaz-Canel's vow to resist any attack. Yet, US officials' late-April Havana visits outlined reform proposals, with Cuba seeking sanctions relief, signaling a diplomatic off-ramp. A failed Senate Democratic resolution on April 29 to require congressional approval for action preserved executive flexibility but underscored domestic opposition. Absent direct triggers like incursions or failed talks, traders view full-scale clash as unlikely despite Guantánamo Bay frictions and Chinese influence concerns.

markets.question: Congress passes Iran war powers resolution by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate pass the same bill, measure, or resolution that seeks to limit U.S. armed forces military action in the recent US/Israel-Iran conflict by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Legislation will qualify as seeking to limit U.S. armed forces military action in the recent US/Israel–Iran conflict if it explicitly seeks to restrict, terminate, or require congressional approval for U.S. armed forces’ hostilities, strikes, deployments, or other military operations against Iran or its proxy forces. Non-binding statements or measures that express disapproval, call for investigation, or otherwise relate to the US/Israel-Iran conflict without seeking to limit military action will not qualify.

A measure amended by either chamber will only qualify if the amended version is subsequently finally passed by both chambers in identical form.

The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Legislation will qualify as seeking to limit U.S. armed forces military action in the recent US/Israel–Iran conflict if it explicitly seeks to restrict, terminate, or require congressional approval for U.S. armed forces’ hostilities, strikes, deployments, or other military operations against Iran or its proxy forces. Non-binding statements or measures that express disapproval, call for investigation, or otherwise relate to the US/Israel-Iran conflict without seeking to limit military action will not qualify.

A measure amended by either chamber will only qualify if the amended version is subsequently finally passed by both chambers in identical form.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects the repeated failure of Democratic-led War Powers resolutions to advance in a Republican-controlled Congress, with the Senate rejecting a sixth such measure yesterday on a 46-51 vote and the House defeating prior attempts by narrow margins like 213-214 in mid-April. These privileged resolutions, invoking section 5(c) of the 1973 War Powers Resolution to direct President Trump to terminate U.S. Armed Forces involvement in hostilities against Iran under Operation Epic Fury, have consistently fallen along party lines amid GOP support for the administration's strategy. The 60-day authorization clock expired May 1 without congressional approval, but Trump officials declared hostilities "terminated" via a fragile ceasefire, reducing urgency. With slim GOP majorities, no bipartisan momentum, and ongoing diplomatic talks, passage by May 31 appears unlikely despite new introductions like Rep. Crow's H.Con.Res.91.

Challenger Mark Lynch holds 13.8% after Paul Dans's April 10 dropout and endorsement consolidated anti-Graham support from the fragmented field, including Dans at 0.8% and Thomas Murphy at 0.1%, amid polls showing Graham leading 43%-29% but with soft base approval. markets.question: Fed rate cut by December 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for December 2026, currently scheduled for December 8-9. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no December meeting takes place by January 7, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects CME FedWatch-implied probabilities showing over 95% odds of no Federal Reserve rate cut at the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, following the April 28-29 decision to hold the federal funds target steady at 3.50%-3.75%. Elevated inflation—March CPI at 3.3% year-over-year and PCE at 3.5% total, 3.2% core—driven by energy shocks from Middle East tensions and tariff effects, has prompted Chair Powell's cautious stance, emphasizing data-dependent policy amid a stable 4.3% unemployment rate and slowing job gains. The 10-year Treasury yield near 4.40% underscores restrictive positioning; traders eye April CPI on May 12 and June FOMC for potential shifts, with year-end cut odds fading below 50% on persistent price pressures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sinaloa Governor Rubén Rocha Moya's May 2 announcement of a requested temporary license from the state congress—submitted amid ongoing U.S. Department of Justice investigations—has solidified trader consensus at 95.5% for his exit by May 31, reflecting the fallout from his April 29 federal indictment on drug trafficking conspiracy charges tied to Sinaloa Cartel leaders. Opposition parties including PAN, PRI, and MC demand his impeachment or resignation, while business groups like Coparmex Sinaloa call for his removal amid rising violence and economic woes; even some Morena senators urge separation from office to facilitate probes. Approval of the license by congress remains the key procedural step, though rejection or withdrawal appears improbable given mounting bipartisan pressure and President Sheinbaum's scrutiny, with extradition risks as a remote wildcard.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle United host Brighton & Hove Albion in a tightly contested Premier League exact-score market, with trader consensus pricing top outcomes like 2-0, 0-0, and 3-1 at 50% implied probabilities amid evenly matched mid-table form. Newcastle sit 14th on 42 points after 33 games (12-6-15, GD -3), hampered by recent losses to Crystal Palace (1-2) and Bournemouth (1-2), plus key absences including Anthony Gordon (hip), Fabian Schär (ankle), Emil Krafth (knee), and Tino Livramento (heavy knock). Brighton, 9th with 47 points (12-11-10, GD +6), also faltered 0-1 at Tottenham, missing Adam Webster (knee), Stefanos Tzimas (knee), James Milner (knock), and Diego Gomez (injury). Home advantage clashes with mutual defensive vulnerabilities and low-scoring trends, keeping probabilities bunched.

markets.question: Will MetaMask launch a token by September 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Metamask officially launches a token by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Metamask, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Partido Novo (NOVO) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to win the most seats in Brazil's 2026 Senate election, reflecting recent state-level polls from Quaest, Datafolha, and others showing PL pré-candidatos leading in pivotal states like Santa Catarina, Rio de Janeiro, Distrito Federal, Rondônia, and Mato Grosso do Sul. Flávio Bolsonaro's direct involvement has enabled "chapa pura" strategies, positioning PL for 24-30 seats per projections, building on its current largest Senate bancada of 15. Opposition momentum surged after the April 29 Senate rejection of President Lula's STF nominee, underscoring hostility toward his allies. UNIÃO Brasil and REPUBLICANOS trail as center-right contenders with competitive slates in Ceará and elsewhere, amid balanced races elsewhere. Party conventions begin in June, with the October 4 vote determining 54 seats.
